features.name: Project scaffold
features.notes: Git repo initialized with .gitignore, .env.example, and docker-compose.yml per foundation plan Task 1. Directories created. No sensors applicable yet — repo has no application code. NEXT_PUBLIC_BETTER_AUTH_URL intentionally added early as a harmless placeholder needed by the Better Auth client config in Task 7. QA approved with reservations; required corrections completed. Security verdict: ADVISORY only. Follow-ups: runtime rejection of placeholder secrets and broader env-file ignore coverage before deployment work.

features.name: Nginx config
features.notes: Nginx reverse-proxy config at nginx/nginx.conf. Proxies / to next:3000 with proper headers. FastAPI not publicly routed. QA verdict: APPROVED. Security verdict: CLEAN. Residual risk: local nginx syntax validation deferred until tooling/runtime is available.

features.name: PostgreSQL schema migration
features.notes: Initial schema created at database/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql. 5 tables: words, cards, review_log, ai_content_cache, user_settings with CHECK, UNIQUE, REFERENCES constraints and 3 indexes for hot query paths. QA verdict: APPROVED. Security verdict: ADVISORY. Follow-ups: enforce review_log.user_id consistency with cards.user_id; define cleanup for soft Better Auth user references; verify FSRS due serialization; later decide provider allowlist and AI cache expiry policy.

features.name: Hebrew seed data and import script
features.notes: Top-20 Biblical Hebrew words in database/seed/words.csv with idempotent importer (seed_words.py). Uses ON CONFLICT (hebrew) DO NOTHING against UNIQUE hebrew column, parameterized queries, DATABASE_URL from env. Default CSV path resolved relative to script location. Connection cleanup via try/finally. Initial QA rejected dead-code idempotency; correction added UNIQUE words.hebrew plus ON CONFLICT (hebrew), fixed the duplicated erets gloss, made the default CSV path script-relative, and added explicit cleanup. QA recheck: APPROVED. Security verdict: CLEAN.

features.name: FastAPI stub
features.notes: Minimal FastAPI stub at backend/. Health check only. Dependencies: fastapi==0.136.1, uvicorn[standard]==0.46.0. Dockerfile uses python:3.14-slim. LiteLLM excluded per ADR-001. QA verdict: APPROVED. Security verdict: CLEAN. Container runtime validation deferred because Docker Compose is unavailable locally.

features.name: Next.js scaffold
features.notes: Next.js 16.2.6 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui (button/card/input/label). Better Auth deps installed. Dockerfile uses node:24-alpine. output: standalone. Build and lint green. QA verdict: APPROVED WITH RESERVATIONS. Security verdict: ADVISORY. Pre-Task 7 follow-ups: remove or justify @better-auth/client pre-alpha dependency, move shadcn to devDependencies, update frontend/CLAUDE.md to reference root contract, and consider non-root runner user before deployment.

features.name: Better Auth configuration
features.notes: Better Auth 1.6.11 configured. Server config in src/lib/auth.ts (PostgreSQL pool, email+password). Browser client in src/lib/auth-client.ts (better-auth/client stable subpath). Catch-all route at src/app/api/auth/[...all]/route.ts. @better-auth/client removed (redundant pre-alpha). shadcn moved to devDeps. frontend/CLAUDE.md links root contract. QA verdict: APPROVED WITH RESERVATIONS; doc corrections completed. Security verdict: CLEAN. Better Auth DB migration deferred until PostgreSQL is available.

features.name: Auth pages
features.notes: Login/register page at frontend/src/app/login/page.tsx. Combined mode toggle (login/register), email+password fields, name field for register, error and loading state, redirects to / on success. Uses authClient.signIn.email and authClient.signUp.email. shadcn Card/Input/Label/Button components. Build and lint green. QA corrections: mode toggle clears stale error; error.message uses ?? fallback. End-to-end flow deferred — Better Auth tables not yet migrated. Follow-up before foundation-010: map raw Better Auth error strings to safe Portuguese messages instead of forwarding server text verbatim. QA verdict: APPROVED WITH RESERVATIONS; corrections completed (clear stale errors on mode toggle; safe undefined-message fallback). Security verdict: ADVISORY. Before foundation-010, replace raw Better Auth error forwarding with safe user-facing Portuguese messages to reduce user-enumeration/info-leak risk.

features.name: Route protection and dashboard shell
features.notes: Route protection now uses frontend/src/proxy.ts, which protects all routes except /login and /api/auth. It checks better-auth session cookies and redirects to /login if missing. Dashboard uses server-side auth.api.getSession. Build and lint pass without the deprecated middleware warning.

features.name: Full stack smoke test
features.notes: Docker Compose smoke test verified live. Added 002_seed_words.sql to auto-seed words via initdb.d. Fixed docker-compose.yml postgres volume path from /var/lib/postgresql/data to /var/lib/postgresql (postgres:18+ convention change). Smoke results: nginx/Next.js → 307 (proxy middleware active), FastAPI /health → {status:ok}, PostgreSQL COUNT(words) → 20. QA verdict: APPROVED. Security verdict: ADVISORY. Follow-ups carried into core-engine: Better Auth tables still require npx better-auth migrate before auth E2E coverage; NEXT_PUBLIC_BETTER_AUTH_URL must be passed as a Docker build arg before non-local deployment; docker-entrypoint-initdb.d seeds only on first named-volume initialization; add explicit secret-handling guidance for AI provider keys before real AI calls; Better Auth raw error forwarding still needs safe Portuguese message mapping.

features.name: Backend infrastructure
features.notes: Core engine plan Task 1. Created backend/db.py (psycopg2 ThreadedConnectionPool, FastAPI dependency, commit/rollback/putconn lifecycle). Created backend/models.py (Word, CardWithContent, NextCardsResponse, ReviewRequest, ReviewResponse — pure Pydantic contracts). Created backend/tests/__init__.py and backend/tests/conftest.py (mock_db and client fixtures with dependency_overrides). Added to requirements.txt: psycopg2-binary==2.9.10, pytest==8.3.4, httpx==0.28.1, pytest-mock==3.14.0. Updated Dockerfile to add build-essential + libpq-dev (psycopg2-binary has no pre-built wheel for Python 3.14 and must be compiled from source). Docker build: PASS. Import smoke test (db_connection, all models): PASS. pytest: 0 collected (conftest only — scaffolding only, no real test modules yet). QA verdict: APPROVED WITH RESERVATIONS. Security verdict: ADVISORY. Hardening follow-up completed: backend/.dockerignore now excludes .env, .env.*, __pycache__/, *.pyc, .pytest_cache/ and related Python/OS junk; follow-up QA verdict: APPROVED; Security verdict: CLEAN. Carry forward: constrain format/rating before core-003; add lock guard around _get_pool() before core-004; defer multi-stage image, non-root user, explicit DATABASE_URL validation, and pydantic pin to pre-production hardening.

features.name: FSRS scheduling service
features.notes: Core engine plan Task 2. Created backend/fsrs_service.py: fsrs_state_to_card, card_to_fsrs_state, schedule_review, determine_format. Created backend/tests/test_fsrs_service.py: 8 tests, all passing. Added fsrs==6.3.1 to requirements.txt (PyPI package name is 'fsrs', not 'py-fsrs'; v6 has no 'reps' field — 'reps' is carried as a synthetic counter in our state dict and set as a dynamic attribute on Card, which is a dataclass without __slots__). TDD cycle confirmed in Docker: red on ImportError, green on all 8. Full suite 8/8, 0 regressions. QA verdict: APPROVED WITH RESERVATIONS. Security verdict: ADVISORY. Carry forward: before core-003, constrain CardWithContent.format and ReviewRequest.rating, and guard fsrs_state_to_card() so missing keys do not overwrite FSRS defaults with None; before core-004, make _get_pool() thread-safe, prevent invalid rating from surfacing as an unhandled KeyError, and validate fsrs_state shape before datetime parsing.

open_decisions: Confirm the initial Hebrew seed list size and source before production import.
Confirm the production AI provider priority order behind the internal provider adapter.
Confirm final VPS hostname and certificate issuance details before deploy work.
Decide how to enforce review_log.user_id consistency with cards.user_id before production data paths rely on it.
